titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Carolyn Davidson Hill Diary and Family Papers
Hill (Carolyn Davidson) Diary and Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Joshua D. Cochran

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday August 28, 2021 at 07:04

did.unittitle: Carolyn Davidson Hill Diary and Family Papers
did.abstract: The Carolyn Davidson Hill diary and family papers consist of the diary Hill kept during the Battle of Shanghai in 1949 as well as photographs and related material on the experiences of Hill, her husband Horace "Hod" Hill, and other workers at the Caltex Oil Terminal. Additional materials in the collection document the Hill's family history, their friends, work, and travel. A letter from a family friend, Paul Bordwell, discusses his experience in Singapore during an anti-colonial riot in December 1950.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is available for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Tracy Hill Leddy and Ann Elizabeth Hill, 2021

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in two series: I. Battle for Shanghai diary and related materials, 1948-1951, 2019. II. Hill family papers, 1914-2021.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Carolyn Davidson Hill (née Carolyn Davidson Allen) was born in Dallas, Texas on October 10, 1911. She attended Southern Methodist University, completing a BA in History and French in 1931 and an MA in Political Science the following year. Initially working as a secretary for several companies and individuals in the Dallas, Texas area, she moved to Washington, D.C., in 1941 and was employed by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation's Division of Research and Statistics.

During the Second World War, Carolyn Davidson Hill worked as a clerk for the United States State Department at several posts in Africa and Asia. In 1943, she was stationed at the Office of Foreign Relief and Rehabilitation Operations in Algiers, Algeria. There she served as a secretary and interpreter for the Chief of Mission. From 1943 to 1945, she was posted in Tehran, Iran with the American Financial Mission. She acted as the secretary for a series of conferences held in Tehran among United States, British, and Iranian officials throughout the war. After the war, Hill was posted to Chongqing, China, where she served from 1945-1946, followed by a posting in Beijing. Her final State Department assignment was in Shanghai, where she worked for the American Consulate General from 1946-1949. In Shanghai, she prepared reports on various financial and political matters. She resigned from the State Department in April 1949.

While in Shanghai, Carolyn Davidson met Horace "Hod" Hill, the superintendent of the Caltex Oil Terminal. Horace Hill was born in 1911 in East Orange, New Jersey and traveled extensively in his youth with his military family. Prior to 1936, Horace was married to Phyllis Abell Hill. From 1939 to 1948, Horace was married to Olivia Whitney Hill, with whom he had two daughters, Tracy (born 1940) and Deborah (born 1942). Horace Hill served in China during the Second World War and began working for the Caltex Petroleum Company in the late 1940s. Carolyn Davidson and Horace Hill married on October 26, 1948.

Living in Shanghai in the late 1940s, Carolyn and Horace Hill experienced the ongoing Chinese Civil War between the Nationalist forces (Kuomintang) and the Communist forces. As the conflict engulfed Shanghai in April 1945, Carolyn Davidson Hill began a diary documenting the experiences and daily life of her, Horace, and Caltex employees over the weeks leading up to the Communist victory in Shanghai in May 1949. The Hills also witnessed the Nationalist bombing of the Anchises, a British freighter in the Huangpu (Whangpoo) River on June 21, 1949, which received international attention.

Carolyn and Horace lived in the Philippines before returning to the United States. The Hills adopted two children, Robert Brinton and Ann Elizabeth. The couple divorced in 1957. After their divorce, Carolyn Davidson Hill lived in California, where she worked for several legal firms and for Stanford University. She died in San Mateo, California on August 10, 1982.

Horace Hill returned to working overseas in Singapore and later settled in Oak Ridge, Tennessee. He married Mary McClees in 1957 and adopted her three children: Hebert, Hugh, and Mary. Horace Hill died in Oak Ridge, Tennessee on December 16, 1989.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Carolyn Davidson Hill Diary and Family Papers (MS 2122). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Carolyn Davidson Hill diary and family papers consist of the diary Hill kept during the Battle of Shanghai in 1949. The diary, photographs, and related material document the experiences of Hill, her husband Horace "Hod" Hill, and other workers at the Caltex Oil Terminal outside of Shanghai as the Nationalist and Communist forces engaged in intense fighting. The diary also discusses the Nationalist bombing of the Anchises, a British freighter in the Huangpu (Whangpoo) River on June 21, 1949. Carolyn subsequently compiled and edited the diary and wrote an introduction to it, which is part of this collection.

The collection also includes correspondence about the Hill's family, friends, and travel. A letter from a family friend, Paul Bordwell, discusses his experience in Singapore during an anti-colonial riot in December 1950. Other materials in the collection include notes written by the family, genealogical research conducted by Horace's children, and family photographs.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright to materials authored or otherwise created by Carolyn Davidson Hill is retained by Ann Elizabeth Hill until January 1, 2050 or until her death, whichever occurs first. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Battle for Shanghai diary and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the original typescript diary kept by Carolyn Davidson Hill during the Battle of Shanghai in Spring 1949. The series also includes additional, subsequent copies edited by Carolyn and other family members; photographs; newspaper clippings; and reports prepared by Carolyn Davidson Hill while working for the United States State Department in China.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Battle for Shanghai diary, original typescript with annotations by Carolyn Davidson Hill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first pages in the folder are a letter from Carolyn Davidson Hill to "Joan" dated February 22, 1949. The diary begins with an entry on April 25, 1949 at 2:00 PM. A gap in the diary exists between May 19, 1949 at 8:15 AM (numbered page 22) and May 25, 1949 at 11:25 AM (numbered page 36). Numbered pages 36 through 49 cover the period of May 25-26, 1949. This span of pages also includes some unnumbered pages (e.g., "Radio messages").
The diary has sporadic entries from June 10-27, 1949. The final entries are from August 8-12, 1949.

did.unittitle: Battle for Shanghai diary, edited by Carolyn Davidson Hill
Battle for Shanghai diary, edited by Carolyn Davidson Hill, with introduction edited by Herbert McClees

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a hand drawn map of the oil facilities along the Huangpu (Whangpoo) River near Shanghai.

did.unittitle: Diary introduction, original typescript with annotations by Carolyn Davidson Hill and edited photocopies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Orginial typescript introduction to the Battle of Shanghai Diary by Carolyn Davidson Hill. The introduction was written after the events of Spring 1949. Folder also includes an edited typescript version of the introduction.

did.unittitle: Horace Hill's exit visa, Chinese currency, and Caltex business cards
Newspaper clipplings
Photographs, Shanghai
The Pootung Poop (Caltex Terminal satirical newsletter)
Research on Caltex Terminal and Chinese Civil War by Herbert McClees
"Soak the rich movement in China" essay written by Carolyn Davidson Hill while at the United States Consulate in Shanghai
"Travel and tourism in China: supplementary report" written by Carolyn Davidson Hill while at the United States Consulate in Shanghai
Wortman, Marc "Sitting on top of liquid dynamite" Time Magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wortman's 2019 article in Time Magazine on the diary, as well as email correspondence with the family and a description of the photographs found in photographs folder.

did.unittitle: Hill family papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the family of Carolyn Davidson Hill and Horace "Hod" Hill. Materials include correspondence, information about their professional work, family photographs, and documentation of their marriage and divorce in 1957. Several folders include explanatory notes added by Horace's children about the family's history.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into three groupings: correspondence, subject files, and family photographs and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Correspondence
Carolyn Davidson Hill letters
Hill family letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters collected by Deborah Hill, daughter of Horace Hill. Correspondence includes a December 1950 letter from family friend Paul Bordwell describing his experiences while in Singapore during the nationwide anti-colonial riots following the Maria Hertogh custody verdict. Other letters include correspondence between Carolyn and Horace and with their friends while in Shanghai.

did.unittitle: Olivia Hill letters and passage to China
Subject files
Carolyn Davidson Hill personal history statement (résumé)
Horace "Hod" Hill personal history

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes added by the family about Horace Hill.

did.unittitle: Horace Hill divorce paperwork
Family photographs and ephemera
Family ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes passports, Japanese currency, and notes about family acquaintance Douglas Blaufarb, who was a Central Intelligence Agency station chief in Laos in the 1960s.

did.unittitle: Family photographs
Photographs of Horace Hill
Retirement certificate for Horace Hill
